key_insight: Local stress redistribution from pre-existing fractures, especially shear-induced changes in normal stress, determines whether an approaching hydraulic fracture deflects, links, or propagates straight.
engineering_relevance: Understanding and controlling near-fracture stress states can improve prediction and steering of hydraulic fractures in reservoir stimulation and subsurface storage.
method: Plane-strain lab tests on intact and pre-fractured PMMA specimens
Digital image correlation measured full-field displacement and strain during loading and fracturing
Coupled poroelastic XFEM simulations reproduced fracture trajectories and curvature
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the lab observations and simulations consistently distinguish pure Mode I growth in intact specimens from mixed-mode HF-NF interaction behavior in fractured specimens.
watch_item: Results are based on PMMA and controlled boundary conditions, so field-scale rock heterogeneity and 3D effects may limit direct transferability.
paper_title: Local Stress Redistribution Controls Interactions between Hydraulic Fractures and Pre-existing Fractures
